doc: v080-0014 — live 3-engine ASR->GDN-LLM->MOSS pipeline, co-resident on Orin NX 16GB
device: orin-nx (orinnx, Linux aarch64, JetPack 6.2 / TRT 10.3, 15656 MB unified)

accuracy_sanity.asr_transcript: 这并不是告别，这是一个篇章的结束，也是新篇章的开始。
accuracy_sanity.llm_response: 这句话表达了旧阶段的终结与新旅程的开启。

coresident_peak_memory.method: MOSS worker held resident + long llm_inference generation running + ASR run concurrently; tegrastats captures the true triple-overlap (not a projection)
coresident_peak_memory.vs_estimate: measured 11062 MB is within the ~8-11.5GB math estimate (top end); 4.5 GB headroom

verdict: PASS — full v0.8.0 ASR->GDN-LLM->MOSS pipeline runs co-resident on Orin NX 16GB. Speed (ASR 4.9s, LLM TTFT 0.53s / 25 tok/s, MOSS TTFA 151ms RTF 0.26), accuracy (correct transcript, coherent LLM reply, intelligible audio), and memory (11062/15656 MB peak, 4.5GB headroom) all acceptable.
notes: Prior attempt did NOT die on a network drop — the GDN export was OOM-killed by the kernel (cgroup global OOM) because the 4 running containers (notably edge-llm-chat-service ~holding several GB unified) left only 5.5 GB available vs the export's ~21 GB virtual / ~2 GB RSS peak under total memory pressure. Stopping seeed-voice + translator + edge-llm-chat-service freed RAM (avail 5.5 -> 11 GB) and the re-export/build/pipeline all completed. All 3 containers restored afterward.

Stale 0.7.1 GDN onnx/engines (the cause of the earlier 'Int4GroupwiseGemmPlugin not found' build-parse failure) were reclaimed; the fresh v0.8.0 onnx parses cleanly ('Successfully created plugin: Int4GroupwiseGemmPlugin').

MOSS produced zero audio in the first (v080-0013-style) pass only because the driver fed it the full 467-token LLM reply, exceeding MOSS's 384-token prefill profile. With a concise LLM reply (system prompt + bounded length) MOSS synthesizes normally (TTFA 151ms, RTF 0.26).
Workspace paths are under ~/edgellm-workspace/qwen35-4b-awq and ~/asr_v080_e2e (not ~/tensorrt-edgellm-workspace/qwen35-4b-awq as the task brief stated).

peak_ram_mb_sequential (5606) from the single-pass driver is max-of-stages (each stage is a separate short-lived process), NOT the co-resident sum; the 11062 MB co-resident figure is the definitive 16GB-fit proof.
